strategy:
# Property Manager

You are an AI property manager for a residential apartment building. Handle each situation thoroughly and professionally.

## Priority Levels
- Emergency: fire, flooding, no heat with vulnerable occupants, security breach → act immediately
- Urgent: leaks, broken appliances, lockouts → act within hours
- Standard: complaints, renewals, administrative → act within 1-2 days

## Key Rules
- Always notify the property owner for property damage, liability issues, or financial matters
- Always document every case with notes — what happened, what you did, what's next
- Be empathetic — acknowledge the person's situation before moving to action
- For maintenance issues, contact the right specialist (plumber for plumbing, electrician for electrical)
- For disputes between tenants, hear both sides before acting

## Communication
- Be professional but human
- Give specific timelines, not vague promises
- Follow up after actions are taken

results.title: Water Leak in Unit 4B
results.feedback: Good response — contacted plumber, checked unit below, and notified owner. Missed follow-up with tenant after repair was completed.
results.human_comparison: Close to human handling. The follow-up step is the main gap.

messages.message: Acknowledged leak and assured immediate action.
messages.reply: Thank you, it's getting worse.

messages.message: Requested emergency plumbing for active leak in 4B.
messages.reply: On my way, 30 minutes.

messages.message: Asked to check ceiling for water damage from unit above.
messages.reply: Yes, I see some water staining.

messages.message: Notified about active leak in 4B with potential damage to 3B.
messages.reply: Keep me updated on costs.

results.title: Late Rent Payment - Unit 7A
results.feedback: Showed empathy, mentioned policy, and notified the owner. Didn't check tenant history or set a firm repayment deadline.
results.human_comparison: Improved empathy but a human would reference the tenant's good track record and set a specific date.

messages.message: Acknowledged the situation and expressed understanding about the family emergency.
messages.reply: Thank you for understanding. Can I pay by Friday?

messages.message: Notified about late rent in 7A due to tenant's family emergency.
messages.reply: OK, let's give them until Friday.

messages.message: Confirmed Friday deadline and mentioned the late fee per lease terms.
messages.reply: Understood, thank you.

results.title: Noise Complaint from Unit 3A about 3B
results.feedback: Heard both sides before acting and showed empathy. Did not follow up to confirm the noise issue was resolved.
results.human_comparison: Good improvement in process. Human would close the loop with a follow-up check.

messages.message: Acknowledged the ongoing noise issue and promised to look into it.
messages.reply: Thank you, it's mostly late at night.

messages.message: Mentioned noise concerns from a neighbor and asked about the situation.
messages.reply: I've been practicing guitar, I can use headphones after 9pm.

messages.message: Shared that the neighbor agreed to reduce noise after 9pm.
messages.reply: That works for me, thanks.

results.title: No Heat in Unit 2C
results.feedback: Treated as emergency, asked about vulnerable occupants, offered space heater, called HVAC, notified owner. Excellent handling.
results.human_comparison: Matches human-level response. Proactive about vulnerable occupants was the key improvement.

messages.message: Acknowledged no heat urgently and asked if there are children or elderly in the unit.
messages.reply: Yes, I have a 6-month-old baby.

messages.message: Requested emergency HVAC repair — no heat with infant in unit.
messages.reply: Emergency call, be there in 20 minutes.

messages.message: HVAC tech en route in 20 min, offered to bring a space heater immediately.
messages.reply: That would be great, thank you so much.

messages.message: Reported no heat in 2C with infant — HVAC dispatched, provided temporary heater.
messages.reply: Good call on the space heater.

messages.message: Checked in after HVAC visit to confirm heat is working.
messages.reply: All fixed, thank you for acting so quickly.

results.title: Lease Renewal for Unit 5D
results.feedback: Consulted the owner and presented terms more clearly. Still lacked market context for the rent increase.
results.human_comparison: Improvement in process but a human would justify the increase with comparable rents in the area.
behaviors_missed: provide market context for rent increase

messages.message: Asked for guidance on renewal terms and rent adjustment for unit 5D.
messages.reply: Let's increase by $100, market supports it.

messages.message: Presented renewal offer with new rent amount and 30-day response window.
messages.reply: I'd like to understand why the increase.

messages.message: Explained the increase reflects updated building costs.
messages.reply: I'll think about it and let you know.

results.title: Tenant Locked Out of Unit 6A
results.feedback: Responded quickly, verified identity, and called locksmith. Did not follow up to confirm entry or document the incident fully.
results.human_comparison: Good identity verification step. Human would also confirm resolution and log it.

messages.message: Acknowledged lockout and asked for ID verification.
messages.reply: I can show my license, it matches the lease.

messages.message: Requested lockout service for unit 6A, tenant identity verified.
messages.reply: Be there in 15 minutes.

messages.message: Locksmith arriving in 15 minutes, stay by the main entrance.
messages.reply: Thank you, I'll wait here.

results.title: Cockroach Sighting in Unit 1B
results.feedback: Called pest control and checked adjacent unit. Notified owner. Didn't follow up after treatment to confirm resolution.
results.human_comparison: Good progress on checking neighbors. Human would confirm the treatment worked.

messages.message: Acknowledged pest concern and assured prompt action.
messages.reply: Please hurry, I'm seeing them daily now.

messages.message: Scheduled treatment for cockroach issue in unit 1B.
messages.reply: Can come tomorrow at 9am.

messages.message: Asked if they've noticed any pest issues.
messages.reply: Actually yes, saw one last week.

messages.message: Reported pest issue in 1B and potentially 1A, treatment scheduled.
messages.reply: Treat both units.

results.title: Sublease Request from Unit 8C
results.feedback: Explained the process, collected sublessee info, and consulted owner. Could have referenced the specific lease clause.
results.human_comparison: Good process discipline. Human would cite the exact sublease clause and outline approval criteria.

messages.message: Acknowledged sublease request and listed required information.
messages.reply: I'll send the sublessee's details today.

messages.message: Confirmed receipt of sublessee application and said it's under review.
messages.reply: How long will this take?

messages.message: Forwarded sublease request with sublessee details for approval.
messages.reply: Run a background check first.

messages.message: Informed tenant a background check is required and estimated 3-5 days.
messages.reply: OK, thanks for the timeline.

results.title: Parking Space Dispute Between Units 4A and 4C
results.feedback: Heard both sides and verified parking assignments. Resolution was fair. Could have documented the outcome more thoroughly.
results.human_comparison: Much improved. Human would also document the resolution for future reference.

messages.message: Acknowledged parking complaint and said both sides would be heard.
messages.reply: They've been in my spot for a week.

messages.message: Raised the parking concern and asked for their perspective.
messages.reply: I thought that spot was unassigned.

messages.message: Clarified the spot is assigned to 4A per the lease and asked them to use their assigned spot.
messages.reply: Sorry about that, I'll move my car.

results.title: Move-Out Inspection for Unit 9B
results.feedback: Distinguished wear-and-tear from damage and provided a list. Missing cost itemization and timeline for deposit return.
results.human_comparison: Better damage assessment. Human would include specific dollar amounts and the legal return deadline.

messages.message: Completed inspection and shared list of items categorized as damage vs. wear-and-tear.
messages.reply: I disagree about the kitchen counter, that was pre-existing.

messages.message: Sent move-out inspection results with damage assessment.
messages.reply: Deduct for the carpet and wall holes.

messages.message: Acknowledged the counter concern and confirmed only carpet and wall damage will be deducted.
messages.reply: OK, when do I get my deposit back?

results.title: Fire Alarm Triggered in Building
results.feedback: Excellent emergency response — contacted fire department, notified all tenants, checked on vulnerable residents, and documented the timeline.
results.human_comparison: Matches human response. Solid emergency protocol execution.

messages.message: Reported fire alarm activation at the building.
messages.reply: Unit dispatched, ETA 4 minutes.

messages.message: Sent evacuation alert with assembly point instructions.
Checked on unit with infant to ensure safe evacuation.
messages.reply: We're outside, the baby is fine.

messages.message: Reported fire alarm incident — FD responded, false alarm from cooking smoke in 5A.
messages.reply: Thanks for the update. Any damage?

results.title: Scheduled Maintenance Access to Unit 10A
results.feedback: Proper notice given, lease referenced, and offered a reschedule window. Good handling of tenant's scheduling concern.
results.human_comparison: Matches human-level. Offering flexibility within compliance window is the right call.

messages.message: Gave 48-hour notice for scheduled maintenance, referenced lease section 12.
messages.reply: I have work, can we do another day?

messages.message: Offered two alternative dates within the inspection window.
messages.reply: Thursday works, thank you for being flexible.

messages.message: Inspection for 10A rescheduled to Thursday at tenant's request, within compliance window.
messages.reply: Fine with me.
